steps.title: Use the discriminant shortcut
steps.content: A quadratic has two distinct real solutions when its discriminant is positive. Here $a=k$, $b=-112$, and $c=16$, so $$(-112)^2-4(k)(16)>0.$$

steps.title: Solve the inequality for k
steps.content: Simplify the inequality: $$12544-64k>0$$ $$12544>64k$$ $$196>k.$$ So $k$ must be less than $196$.

steps.title: Choose the greatest integer
steps.content: Since $k$ must be an integer and must be less than $196$, the greatest possible value is $195$. Check: $12544-64(195)=64>0$, so there are two distinct real solutions. The answer is 195.
